text: The surgeon performs laparoscopic dissection to separate severe adhesions between the abdominal wall and the omentum, ensuring safe access to the layer containing the target lesion.

The surgeon exposes the inferior vena cava and the left renal vein behind the duodenum and head of the pancreas. Dissection is performed around the left renal vein, revealing the superior mesenteric artery and associated nerve tissue. A metastatic lymph node, approximately two centimeters in size, is exposed, surrounded by the pancreatic parenchyma, polar vein, right crux of the diaphragm, and liver caudate lobe.
The surgeon clips the feeding artery of the metastatic lymph node and cuts the artery, ensuring minimal bleeding during the resection of the lymph node.
